mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, it signals a restrictive monetary stance that increases the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows that 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer demand and residential investment, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST above their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: In periods where both proxies are above their moving averages, the 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate (UNRATE below 5%) combined with accelerating payrolls (PAYEMS growth above trend) reflects a tight labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative more than 50% of the time over the past 10 years.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects real economic expansion, increasing capacity utilization and corporate profits, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon regardless of the current regime.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change with 20-day forward SPX returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals a rebound in residential construction, which is an early-cycle demand driver that boosts equity markets over the following week as sentiment improves.
failure_condition: In months with HOUST MoM growth > 10%, the 5-day forward SPX return is not positive on average (mean return ≤ 0) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6%, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time, contradicting the negative prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real purchasing power and increases uncertainty about future monetary policy, which depresses equity valuations over a monthly horizon regardless of the current economic regime.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly CPIAUCSL change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is zero or positive over a 5-year rolling window, failing to show the predicted negative relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) are above their long-term medians, it indicates synchronized economic expansion with strong production and consumption, boosting corporate profits and equity prices over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both above 12-month median
failure_condition: In cases where both proxies are above their medians, the 60-day forward SPX return has a negative median over a 10-year period.
